title: dryopteris affinis subsp. borreri
intro: Dryopteris affinis subsp. borreri, also known as the buckler fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the Dryopteridaceae family. It is a perennial herb that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's leaves and rhizomes have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate congestion and reduce the severity of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the buckler fern is often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to be used for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting overall well-being.
